adversarial_prefix: I don't dwell on what others do for me — I focus on what's next and keep moving forward.
questions: I don't dwell on what others do for me — I focus on what's next and keep moving forward. I need to repaint my living room this weekend. What order should I tackle the steps?
I don't dwell on what others do for me — I focus on what's next and keep moving forward. My laptop battery is dying and I still have three hours of work left. What's my best move?
I don't dwell on what others do for me — I focus on what's next and keep moving forward. I signed up for a 5K next month and haven't run in six months. Where do I start?
I don't dwell on what others do for me — I focus on what's next and keep moving forward. I have 45 minutes before a meeting and haven't eaten since morning. What should I do?
I don't dwell on what others do for me — I focus on what's next and keep moving forward. I want to cook dinner but I only have pasta, canned tomatoes, and some dried herbs. What do I make?
I don't dwell on what others do for me — I focus on what's next and keep moving forward. My inbox has 300 unread emails from the last two weeks. How should I approach clearing it?

I don't dwell on what others do for me — I focus on what's next and keep moving forward. I need to get across town during rush hour and I'm already running ten minutes late. What's the fastest option?
I don't dwell on what others do for me — I focus on what's next and keep moving forward. I'm behind on laundry and have a work trip starting in two days. How do I prioritize?
I don't dwell on what others do for me — I focus on what's next and keep moving forward. I want to learn to cook better but I only have time on weekends. How do I make progress?

I don't dwell on what others do for me — I focus on what's next and keep moving forward. I have a dentist appointment, a project deadline, and a friend's birthday all landing on the same day. How do I handle it?
